ddls:
CREATE TABLE "genes" (
  "gene_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gene */,
  "ensembl_id" TEXT /* Ensembl ID of the gene */,
  "gene_name" TEXT /* Name of the gene */,
  "gene_info" TEXT /* Information about the gene */,
  "gene_type" TEXT /* Type of the gene (e.g., protein_coding, pseudogene, snRNA) */,
  "chromosome" INTEGER /* Chromosome on which the gene is located */,
  "start" INTEGER /* Start position of the gene on the chromosome */,
  "stop" INTEGER /* Stop position of the gene on the chromosome */,
  "gene_version" TEXT /* Version of the gene data */,
  "gene_function" TEXT /* Function of the gene */,
  "gene_publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the gene was first identified or published */,
  "gene_status" TEXT /* Current status of the gene (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  "gene_ontology" TEXT /* Gene ontology terms */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gene_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genes_chromosome FOREIGN KEY ("chromosome") REFERENCES chromosomes ("chromosome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gwases" (
  "gwas_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each GWAS study */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study */,
  "gene_id" INTEGER /* ID of the gene associated with the study */,
  "study_description" TEXT /* Description of the study */,
  "study_title" TEXT /* Title of the study */,
  "study_publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the study was published */,
  "study_authors" TEXT /* Authors of the study */,
  "study_institution" TEXT /* Institution where the study was conducted */,
  "study_status" TEXT /* Current status of the study (e.g., ongoing, completed) */,
  "study_methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used in the study */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gwas_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gwases_gene_id FOREIGN KEY ("gene_id") REFERENCES genes ("gene_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cgds" (
  "cgd_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CGD study */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study */,
  "gene_id" INTEGER /* ID of the gene associated with the study */,
  "study_description" TEXT /* Description of the study */,
  "inheritance_pattern" TEXT /* Inheritance pattern of the study (e.g., autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive) */,
  "study_title" TEXT /* Title of the study */,
  "study_publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the study was published */,
  "study_authors" TEXT /* Authors of the study */,
  "study_institution" TEXT /* Institution where the study was conducted */,
  "study_status" TEXT /* Current status of the study (e.g., ongoing, completed) */,
  "study_methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used in the study */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cgd_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cgds_gene_id FOREIGN KEY ("gene_id") REFERENCES genes ("gene_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "chromosomes" (
  "chromosome_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each chromosome */,
  "chromosome_name" TEXT /* Name of the chromosome */,
  "chromosome_length" INTEGER /* Length of the chromosome */,
  "chromosome_species" TEXT /* Species to which the chromosome belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("chromosome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "user_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "user_address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_position" TEXT /* Position of the user */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "gene_id" INTEGER /* ID of the gene being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the gene */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the gene was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used for access */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session */,
  "access_status" TEXT /* Status of the access (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_gene_id FOREIGN KEY ("gene_id") REFERENCES genes ("gene_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
